requirement: The dashboard shall present camera content organized into at least three named views: "Streams", "Stills", and "Map".
The dashboard shall provide a control that lets the user make exactly one view active at a time.
rationale: Single active view bounds resource use.
notes: Tab control.

requirement: The dashboard shall display, in each view's tab label, the count of cameras in that view.
The dashboard shall mount media (streams, iframes, images) only for the currently active view.
notes: Rationale for SYS-002.

requirement: For each camera classified as an HLS stream, the dashboard shall play the stream using an in-browser HLS player.
rationale: SLU Pixelcaster m3u8 has no native Chromium playback.

requirement: The dashboard shall use the bundled JavaScript HLS library for HLS playback whenever that library reports itself supported, using native HLS only as a fallback.
rationale: canPlayType('...mpegurl')='maybe' in Chromium is unreliable; library is authoritative.
notes: Defect fix during MVP; see design decision D-004.

requirement: For each camera classified as an embeddable stream, the dashboard shall render it within an inline frame.
The dashboard shall begin playback of an HLS grid stream when its card enters the viewport.
notes: IntersectionObserver autostart; observed flaky under headless automation only.

requirement: While a live stream card is displayed and visible, the dashboard shall maintain continuous playback of that stream.
notes: IMPLIED requirement, not implemented. wetmet iframes stall to black after minutes -> ANOM-001. Illustrates a needs->requirements gap.

requirement: For each camera classified as a still image, the dashboard shall display the image and shall refresh it at a fixed interval not exceeding 30 s while the document is visible.
rationale: Periodic sources update every ~1-5 min; refresh keeps it current.
notes: Interval value (20 s) chosen by implementer -> TBR-002.

requirement: The dashboard shall append a cache-busting query parameter to each still-image request.
rationale: Force a fresh frame past HTTP caches.

requirement: For each camera classified as a link-out, the dashboard shall present a control that opens the camera's source page in a new browsing context.
The Map view shall display an interactive map with a marker at the position of each camera that has geographic coordinates.

For each camera that has both an azimuth and a field-of-view, the Map view shall render a field-of-view wedge spanning azimuth +/- (fov/2) using compass bearings (0 deg = North, increasing clockwise).
notes: Verified against dummy: left 359/right 69 -> az 34 NE.

requirement: Upon user selection of a camera marker, the Map view shall open that camera's stream in a modal overlay.
The Map view shall present, for each marker, a tooltip that identifies the camera.
notes: Current content (name+az+fov+elev) under change -> CR-007.

requirement: The dashboard shall compute field-of-view wedge vertices using a geodesic destination model on a spherical-earth approximation.
The Map view shall render base-map tiles retrieved from OpenStreetMap at runtime.
notes: Introduces a runtime network dependency for the map only.

requirement: The Map view shall display attribution crediting OpenStreetMap.
notes: Compliance requirement.

requirement: On entering the Map view, the dashboard shall set the map extent to include all geolocated cameras.
rationale: Show the full set without manual zoom.

requirement: The Map view shall draw each field-of-view wedge to a fixed ground length of 1600 m unless a per-camera length is specified.
rationale: Make wedges visible at metro zoom for elevated long-range cams.
notes: Fixed length is a placeholder; per-camera landmark radius desired -> TBR-003 / CR-011.

requirement: The dashboard shall visually indicate the operational status of any camera whose status is not "live".
notes: Status taxonomy semantics undefined -> TBR-006.

requirement: The dashboard shall obtain its camera dataset from a single JSON document retrieved at runtime.
The camera dataset shall be generated from a human-editable CSV source of truth by a conversion utility.
notes: cameras.csv + locations.csv -> convert.py -> cameras.json.

requirement: The conversion utility shall derive the centerline azimuth and total field-of-view from left/right FOV-edge headings when both are provided, sweeping clockwise from left to right and handling 0/360 wraparound.
rationale: Let the stakeholder supply edge headings instead of doing trig.
notes: Unit-tested (6 cases).

requirement: The conversion utility shall exclude any source row marked "skip" from the generated dataset.
rationale: Keep dead/non-camera rows out of the app while retaining them in source.

requirement: For each geolocated camera, the dataset shall carry latitude and longitude, and optionally azimuth, field-of-view, camera elevation (m ASL), and ground elevation (m ASL).
rationale: Capture geo + elevation for map and future viewshed.

requirement: The conversion utility shall associate location data to a camera by exact name match, and shall treat an unmatched location row that carries a render type and URL as a standalone map-only marker.
rationale: Decouple geo file from camera source; allow test pins.

requirement: Each camera record shall carry a render classification of exactly one of {image, hls, iframe, link}.
The dashboard shall retrieve each camera's media directly from its origin server via the viewing browser, without a server-side proxy.
notes: Consequence: cross-origin CORS/mixed-content constraints apply.

requirement: The dashboard shall request cross-origin media using a privacy-preserving referrer policy where the platform supports it.
rationale: Avoid leaking the dashboard URL to origins.
notes: referrerpolicy=no-referrer on images.

requirement: The dashboard shall be deployable as static files with no server-side runtime and no build step.
notes: Design decision D-001 (no-build static).

requirement: The dashboard shall bundle all third-party client libraries within the deployed artifact and shall not depend on an external CDN at runtime for scripts or stylesheets.
notes: hls.js and Leaflet vendored. Note: OSM tiles remain a runtime dependency (SYS-045).

requirement: The dashboard shall render without horizontal page overflow at viewport widths of 320 px and greater.
notes: 320 px threshold chosen by implementer -> TBD-004.

requirement: The dashboard shall adapt its color scheme to the viewer's light/dark preference.
The dashboard shall lay out camera cards in a grid that reflows with viewport width.
The system shall be publishable via GitHub Pages served from the default branch root.
A push to the default branch shall result in publication of the updated site.
The dashboard shall be deployable to an arbitrary static host by copying its files, using relative asset paths only.
The camera dataset shall be limited to cameras within the St. Louis metropolitan region.
notes: OPEN: boundary undefined (TBD-001); dataset currently includes feeds ~80-130 km out (Cuba, Farmington). Not currently verifiable.
